texto: The evening's light, silvery, casts its dull brightness onto the trees--trees gelid in this blue light of winter. But whiteness dominates with the pines and evergreens steeped in vibrant grades of silver. I hear notes in the mist, like silvery chattering, coins in a pocket, the jangle of keys. Pg 217
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a S.K. Kalsi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “The evening's light, silvery, casts its dull brightness onto the trees--trees gelid in this blue …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, delerium, dementia pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dementia e hallucinations.

texto: I would think of certain winter nights when he wedged himself between Nona and me in bed, a furtive warmth embedded in his skin already tinctured with virginal earth and milk and possibility, or how that peculiar scent common to all small children before the age of five--sunshine sweetened hair, a nascent woodsiness in him exuding youthful exuberance--gripped us, suspended us eighties in the sense that our hope, our very survival, depended on the fulfillment of this child's dreams. How I took those years I spent for granted, believing them unalterable? pg. 9
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de S.K. Kalsi que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “I would think of certain winter nights when he wedged himself between Nona and me in bed, a furti…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, childhood, memories, com foco em boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: Losing myself interests me. The fertile topsoil interests me, sprawling beneath a light dusting of snow, and the snow that crams the trunks and branches of the pines and elms and redwoods, having frozen up their roots, subdues me to consider life and death. What lurks beneath the ground? Surely dead seeds and frozen worms reside deep below that earth, and surely all those presentiments of life lying dormant, dead or dying, scattered and mute, like memories.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a S.K. Kalsi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Losing myself interests me. The fertile topsoil interests me, sprawling beneath a light dusting of snow, and the snow that crams the trunks and branches of the pines and elms and redwoods, having frozen up their roots, subdues me to consider life and death. What lurks beneath the ground? Surely dead seeds and frozen worms reside deep below that earth, and surely all those presentiments of life lying dormant, dead or dying, scattered and mute, like memories.
